description:
Hong Kong would be “a perfect venue for Formula One”. ⁠
⁠
This is what Hongkonger Frank Yu Siu-fung, who worked on the successful bid to bring the elite motorsport to Thailand, says. ⁠
⁠
Bangkok is expected to be part of the F1 schedule from 2028, joining Japan, Singapore and Shanghai as Asian stops on an increasingly congested calendar. ⁠
⁠
“The Sevens is great, we had Formula E, but other than World Cups, there is nothing like Formula One," he said, adding that making a bid is a "no-brainer".⁠
⁠
📷️: Sam Tsang⁠
⁠
Go to link in bio to read more. ⁠

description:
Last Sunday (3 March), Thai influencer Nattarada “Chopluem” Thampaya learned she fell for a ticket scam after arriving at the National Stadium for Taylor Swift’s concert that day. 

The influencer had flown out of Bangkok that same day with her brother and seven other concertgoers. 

However, eight other people had ‘tickets’ to the same seats. Staff at the stadium then cancelled the seats and instructed the fans who were there to leave the venue. 

In light of the incident, Thailand’s Cyber Crime Investigation Bureau urged all Thai residents who were victims of ticket scams to report to the Thai police.

The seller in turn released a statement, declaring that they’re gathering evidence to assist with investigations.

📷: chopluem on TikTok and @chopluem on Instagram

latest_comments.comments: Ticket should have been verified with id nowadays, or else, it’s so easy for resellers out there to sell back second hand tickets. So many ppl couldn’t go to concert cause of those resellers and worst is those scammers who cheat other people’s money and feelings! God damn! Should have come up with a better verification system like using identity card or something and one id can only buy 1 tix. Or each tix must verify with their own id so it’s easy to identify legit tickets on the spot!
First we thought this won't happen in Singapore and yet now it does. Hear the scammer is a local too. Xia suay

This is not a new story and happening very often in Asia. Mainly because western artists only have live in limited country in Asia, meanwhile there are million times more demand for the concerts compare to western lives. Scalpers become more prominent. And these scalpers are not just from Singapore. But many neighboring countries as well.

description:
Here are my top 6 photo spots in Bangkok, whether you’re a creator or just travelling

Below are the must-visit locations plus my favourite times to shoot them, with some ideas for where to create from:

Number 01 - Wat Benchamabophit
The main gate so you’re head on, this way you can capture a symmetrical shot plus include yourself as a subject. Head here before 8am to avoid the entrance fee and the crowds.
 Number 02 - Chinatown
Good during the day and night, especially along Yaowarat road as it lights up with all the neon signs! Check out all of the markets in the side streets for amazing street photography.
 Number 03 - Phra Buddha
The big Buddha viewpoint across the canal is one of my favourite spots here. Keep your eyes peeled on the walk there as the Buddha pops through buildings, walkways and trees - a compositional dream. Late afternoon or early morning is best for that golden hour glow
 Number 04 - Wat Ratchanatdaram
Golden hour wins again! Standing on the temple’s corners gives it this beautiful, powerful aura but also the grounds are huge so look for framing opportunities in the surrounding buildings.
 Number 05 - Talat Phlu Train
Late afternoon, you guessed it…golden hour! Check out Soi Thoet Thai 33 street to see the train cross through the main building before heading to Talat Phlu Old Station.
 Number 06 - Wat Arun
Anytime is great, with the best viewpoint across the river, near the Grand Palace. Also wander the bustling streets near the Palace as the alleyways create the perfect frame.

⚡Save this post so you have the list ready for your trip!
 What do you think of this post?

description:
PS - Comment “GUIDE” and I’ll send you the full version of my 3-Day Bangkok Itinerary, including a FREE route map 🍤🥭🇹🇭♥️🗺️

Many people skip Bangkok when landing in Thailand on the way to the islands in the south, and I think this is a mistake! ❌

Bangkok is much more than backpacking districts and all sorts of crazy things you’ve seen in The Hangover 2. It’s a cool city with vibrant cultural heritage, banging food, and an incredible blend of old and new ✨

3-5 days is the perfect time to spend in the city, and this guide is a good starting point 🫶

description:
Spots in Bangkok, Thailand 🇹🇭 tourists usually miss! 
Instead of visiting Bangkok’s tourist hotspots, I took a moment to explore the lesser known streets and neighborhoods. And, guess what!? It completly surprised me. I never saw this side of Bangkok the first time I visited over 10 years ago. I truly loved the creative streets, unique shops, and delicious food I discovered this time around.

Have you ever visited a place and not loved it the first time, but then returned and completely fell in love? That’s exactly how this trip felt for me.
 I thought I’d grow tired of the city and have lots of downtime to work, but it was completly the opposite. I wish I could have even stayed longer to discover more of it’s hidden gems.

Save this for your next Bangkok trip 🤍

Comment ‘guide’ and I’ll dm you my blog posts on Thailand when ready!

—
#hiddengems #ThingsToDo #BangkokItinerary #VisitBangkok

description:
Saturday is Fleet Poster Day!
Have a look at the versatile fleet of Thai Airways (including Nok Air and Thai Smile). They are operating the King of Skies (Airbus A380) as well as the Queen of Skies (Boeing 747). Actually they're going to replace their Boeing 747 with Airbus' and Boeings next generation aircraft Airbus A350 and Boeing 787 Dreamliners. I think the Airbus A380 will get retired soon as well. Have you ever went with Thai Airways? Tell me which aircraft you used - the variety of Thai aircraft in Europe is very high. Zurich is served with the Boeing 747-400, Frankfurt often gets the honour of the Airbus A380 and Munich-Spotters can catch the 777.
Tell me your favorite airline in the comments.
Thanks to www.jcai.dk, www.flyingcarpet75.com and juergenbaumbusch.de

latest_comments.comments:
⭐ 1/5 – Disappointing and Confusing Experience
I booked Room Casa Gracia through Trip.com because the booking confirmation clearly displayed a free cancellation policy until May 27, 2026. This was one of the main reasons I chose to make the reservation.
Later, due to circumstances beyond my control, I needed to cancel the booking. During the refund process, I received contradictory information. The hotel stated that my cancellation request was submitted after the free cancellation deadline, which confirms that a free cancellation policy existed. However, Trip.com later informed me that the reservation had been made under a non-refundable policy from the beginning.
As a customer, this situation was extremely confusing. I provided screenshots, booking confirmations, emails, and other evidence showing the cancellation terms displayed at the time of booking. Despite this, I never received a clear explanation as to why the hotel and Trip.com were giving different versions of the same reservation.
I also explained that my visa expiration date prevented me from completing the trip legally, and I acted in good faith throughout the process. Unfortunately, my concerns were not properly addressed, and my refund request was denied.
What disappointed me the most was the lack of transparency and consistency. Customers should be able to rely on the booking conditions presented to them and receive clear answers when disputes arise.
Based on my experience, I cannot recommend this service until better accountability and communication are provided.
Bangkok gets even better when you find a great hotel deal to match the trip. Amazing how affordable a 5-star stay can be with a little planning.

description:
Se puede ver el King Power Mahanakhon de Bangkok en Tailandia.

#Bangkok #Tailandia #worldanimox #turismo #Mahanakhon

Bangkok in 5 Days 🇹🇭✨

From golden temples and floating markets to rooftop cafés and vibrant night streets, this 5-day Bangkok itinerary captures the energy, culture, and flavors of Thailand’s capital.

Day 1 — Bangkok Icons 🛕

Start your Bangkok adventure exploring the Grand Palace, Wat Pho, Wat Arun, and scenic river views along the Chao Phraya.

Day 2 — Markets & Street Food 🍜

Dive into Bangkok’s famous street food scene with colorful markets, Chinatown alleys, local snacks, and lively night markets.

Day 3 — Modern Bangkok 🌃

Experience the modern side of the city with shopping districts, rooftop cafés, glowing skyline views, and Bangkok’s vibrant nightlife.

Day 4 — Floating Markets & Culture 🚤

Discover traditional Thai culture through floating markets, canal boat rides, local temples, and authentic Thai dining experiences.

Day 5 — Hidden Bangkok 🌿

Slow down your final day exploring peaceful neighborhoods, cozy cafés, green parks, and beautiful riverside sunset spots.

Bangkok is a city where tradition, food, nightlife, and culture blend together into unforgettable travel experiences. Save this itinerary for your future Thailand adventure ✨

#Bangkok #ThailandTravel #BangkokItinerary #VisitThailand #minimalbkkwatch
Save this Bangkok Night Market Guide #bangkok #bangkokthailand #bangkoktravel #bangkoknightlife

description:
Chinatown in Bangkok during the sunset. There’s a magical moment when the sun goes down and everything starts to light up. The traffic that never stops, the smell of street food in every street, motorbikes and tuk-tuk flowing everywhere, everyday life moving on in the middle of the chaos. It’s a city you love exactly for all these things.

#bangkok

description:
🇹🇭 20 THINGS TO DO IN BANGKOK, THAILAND (with prices!💰) ⬇️ 

💾 SAVE & LIKE this Bangkok guide for your trip!💛

Bangkok is packed with incredible temples, rooftop bars, street food, markets, culture and unforgettable experiences, making it one of the best cities to visit in the world! 🌍✨

Explore Bangkok’s most iconic temple attractions, from the stunning Wat Arun and historic Wat Pho,  to the magnificent Grand Palace! 😍

Take in breathtaking city views from The King Power Mahanakhon SkyWalk 🌇, discover Bangkok’s famous rooftop bar scene at Tichuca, Akara Sky Hanuman and Tulum Sky Bar 🍸, and wander through the beautiful new Dusit Central Park 🌳

Experience local life on a traditional Bangkok canal tour 🚤, visit the charming Artist’s House & do some crafts🎨, and watch the electric atmosphere of Muay Thai at Rajadamnern Stadium 🥊! 

Shop at the luxury riverside mall ICONSIAM 🛍️, hunt for bargains at Chatuchak Weekend Market 🛒, and explore the vibrant streets of Chinatown 🍜, Song Wat Road ✨ and the legendary Khao San Road 🍻.

For even more fun, challenge your friends at Topgolf Bangkok ⛳, test your skills in an escape room 🔐 and enjoy a sunset cruise along the Chao Phraya River 🚢🌅

We hope you found this post helpful! 👏🏻🇹🇭
